frameworks.name: individualized electric field modeling
frameworks.one_line: anatomically realistic head models simulate per-subject tDCS field distribution and focality

frameworks.name: multiple linear regression prediction
frameworks.one_line: combines pre-tDCS behavior and field focality to predict post-tDCS outcome

frameworks.name: visual crowding
frameworks.one_line: peripheral orientation discrimination is impaired by flanking distractors

core_question: Can individualized electric field modeling combined with pre-tDCS behavioral performance predict variability in tDCS effects on visual crowding?
key_assumptions: tDCS-induced electric field over visual cortex modulates orientation discrimination
individual anatomy meaningfully shapes delivered field focality
pre-tDCS performance carries information about post-tDCS response
manipulations: stimulation montage (bipolar tDCS vs 4×1 HD-tDCS vs sham)
target context (isolated vs crowded peripheral target)
pre- vs post-stimulation orientation discrimination threshold
key_findings: HD-tDCS, but not bipolar tDCS, significantly alleviated visual crowding
tDCS effect variability was reliably predicted by pre-tDCS performance and field focality
interpretations: integrating individualized field modeling with baseline behavior accounts for inter-subject tDCS variability
focal HD-tDCS over visual cortex is more effective than bipolar montage for crowding alleviation
